description: Foundation layer (P0): project scaffolding, JSON-RPC transport, exceptions, config, models, task/project SDK resources, Click CLI skeleton, output formatters, and task/project CLI commands.

userStories.title: Project scaffolding and packaging setup
userStories.description: Create the full project skeleton: pyproject.toml with hatchling build, src/ layout with kanboard/ and kanboard_cli/ packages, all __init__.py files per the target directory structure, Makefile with install/lint/test/coverage targets, MIT LICENSE file, and CHANGELOG.md.
acceptanceCriteria: pyproject.toml exists with hatchling build backend, name='kanboard-cli', version='0.1.0', requires-python='>=3.11'
Dependencies: httpx>=0.27, click>=8.1, rich>=13.0, tomli-w>=1.0
Dev dependencies: pytest>=8.0, pytest-httpx>=0.30, ruff>=0.4, coverage>=7.0
Entry point: kanboard = kanboard_cli.main:cli
src/kanboard/ and src/kanboard_cli/ directories exist with __init__.py files
src/kanboard/resources/ directory exists with __init__.py
src/kanboard_cli/commands/ directory exists with __init__.py
src/kanboard_cli/workflows/ directory exists with __init__.py
tests/, tests/unit/, tests/unit/resources/, tests/cli/, tests/integration/ directories exist with __init__.py or conftest.py as appropriate
Makefile has install, lint, test, coverage targets
MIT LICENSE file exists
CHANGELOG.md exists
pip install -e '.[dev]' succeeds
ruff check . passes with no errors
pytest runs and discovers test directory (0 tests collected is OK)

userStories.title: Exception hierarchy
userStories.description: Create src/kanboard/exceptions.py with the typed exception hierarchy: KanboardError (base), KanboardConfigError, KanboardConnectionError, KanboardAuthError, KanboardAPIError (with KanboardNotFoundError and KanboardValidationError subclasses), and KanboardResponseError. Each exception carries structured context attributes and produces a human-readable str().
acceptanceCriteria: KanboardError is the base exception class
KanboardConfigError accepts message and field attributes
KanboardConnectionError accepts message, url, and cause attributes
KanboardAuthError accepts message and http_status attributes
KanboardAPIError accepts message, method, and code attributes
KanboardNotFoundError is a subclass of KanboardAPIError with resource and identifier attributes
KanboardValidationError is a subclass of KanboardAPIError
KanboardResponseError accepts message and raw_body attributes
All exceptions are importable from kanboard (re-exported in __init__.py)
str() on each exception produces a human-readable message
Unit tests verify construction and string representation of every exception type
All public classes have docstrings
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Configuration system
userStories.description: Build src/kanboard/config.py with the KanboardConfig frozen dataclass and resolve() classmethod implementing layered config resolution: TOML config file → environment variables → CLI flags. Support named profiles, export path constants (CONFIG_DIR, CONFIG_FILE, WORKFLOW_DIR), and include get_workflow_config() for workflow plugin config.
acceptanceCriteria: KanboardConfig is a frozen dataclass with url, token, profile, output_format fields
resolve() classmethod loads ~/.config/kanboard/config.toml via tomllib
Profile selection order: CLI flag > KANBOARD_PROFILE env var > settings.default_profile in config file
Field resolution order for each field: CLI arg > env var > config file value
Missing required url or token raises KanboardConfigError with actionable message
CONFIG_DIR, CONFIG_FILE, WORKFLOW_DIR constants are exported
get_workflow_config(name) loads [workflows.<name>] section from config file
Gracefully handles missing config file (falls back to env vars / CLI flags)
Unit tests cover: full resolution chain, each override layer, missing config file, missing required fields, profile selection, workflow config loading
All public methods have docstrings and full type hints
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 because config raises KanboardConfigError.

userStories.title: JSON-RPC transport layer
userStories.description: Build src/kanboard/client.py with KanboardClient implementing JSON-RPC 2.0 transport over httpx. Includes call() for single requests, batch() for batch requests, context manager support, and proper exception mapping for HTTP and JSON-RPC errors. Resource accessors will be added by later stories.
acceptanceCriteria: KanboardClient.__init__ accepts url, token, and optional timeout (default 30.0)
Uses httpx.Client with HTTP Basic Auth (username='jsonrpc', password=token)
call(method, **params) sends JSON-RPC 2.0 request and returns parsed 'result' value
batch(calls) sends array of JSON-RPC requests and returns list of results
JSON-RPC error responses raise KanboardAPIError with method and code
HTTP 401/403 raises KanboardAuthError with http_status
Connection failures (httpx.ConnectError, etc.) raise KanboardConnectionError
Malformed/non-JSON responses raise KanboardResponseError with raw_body
DEBUG-level logging for request method and response
Context manager (__enter__/__exit__) and close() properly clean up httpx client
Unit tests with pytest-httpx covering: successful call, successful batch, JSON-RPC error, HTTP 401, HTTP 403, connection error, malformed response, timeout
All public methods have docstrings and full type hints
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: SDK response models — core models and helpers
userStories.description: Create src/kanboard/models.py with helper functions (_parse_date, _int) and the core dataclass models: Task, Project, Column, Swimlane, Comment, Subtask, User, Category. Each model has a from_api(data) classmethod that handles Kanboard's type inconsistencies.
acceptanceCriteria: _parse_date() handles None, empty string, '0', 0, Unix timestamps (int/str), 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM', 'YYYY-MM-DD' formats and returns datetime | None
_int() coerces string/int/None values to int (handles Kanboard returning '0' as string)
Task dataclass covers all fields from getTask API response with from_api() classmethod
Project dataclass covers all fields from getProjectById API response with from_api()
Column dataclass with from_api()
Swimlane dataclass with from_api()
Comment dataclass with from_api()
Subtask dataclass with from_api()
User dataclass with from_api()
Category dataclass with from_api()
All from_api() methods use .get() with sensible defaults for missing keys
All models importable from kanboard (re-exported in __init__.py)
Unit tests with sample API payloads for each model and edge cases for helpers
All public classes and methods have docstrings
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: SDK response models — extended models
userStories.description: Add remaining dataclass models to src/kanboard/models.py: Tag, Link, TaskLink, ExternalTaskLink, Group, ProjectFile, TaskFile, Action. Follow the same from_api() pattern established in US-005.
acceptanceCriteria: Tag dataclass with from_api() covering getTagsByProject response fields
Link dataclass with from_api() covering getLinkById response fields
TaskLink dataclass with from_api() covering getTaskLinkById response fields
ExternalTaskLink dataclass with from_api() covering getExternalTaskLinkById response fields
Group dataclass with from_api() covering getGroup response fields
ProjectFile dataclass with from_api() covering getProjectFile response fields
TaskFile dataclass with from_api() covering getTaskFile response fields
Action dataclass with from_api() covering getActions response fields
All models importable from kanboard (added to __init__.py exports)
Unit tests with sample API payloads for each new model
All public classes and methods have docstrings
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Tasks resource module — SDK
userStories.description: Implement src/kanboard/resources/tasks.py with TasksResource class covering all 14 task API methods. Wire into KanboardClient as client.tasks. This establishes the resource-module pattern all subsequent resources will follow.
acceptanceCriteria: TasksResource class with __init__(self, client) pattern
create_task(title, project_id, **kwargs) -> int; raises KanboardAPIError on False
get_task(task_id) -> Task; raises KanboardNotFoundError on None
get_task_by_reference(project_id, reference) -> Task; raises KanboardNotFoundError on None
get_all_tasks(project_id, status_id=1) -> list[Task]; returns empty list on False/None
get_overdue_tasks() -> list[Task]
get_overdue_tasks_by_project(project_id) -> list[Task]
update_task(task_id, **kwargs) -> bool; raises KanboardAPIError on False
open_task(task_id) -> bool
close_task(task_id) -> bool
remove_task(task_id) -> bool
move_task_position(project_id, task_id, column_id, position, swimlane_id) -> bool
move_task_to_project(task_id, project_id, **kwargs) -> bool
duplicate_task_to_project(task_id, project_id, **kwargs) -> int
search_tasks(project_id, query) -> list[Task]
Wired into KanboardClient as self.tasks = TasksResource(self)
All methods fully type-hinted with typed returns
All public methods have docstrings
Unit tests for every method with pytest-httpx mocking
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: US-002 (exceptions) is transitively required via US-004.

userStories.title: Projects resource module — SDK
userStories.description: Implement src/kanboard/resources/projects.py with ProjectsResource class covering all 14 project API methods. Wire into KanboardClient as client.projects. Follow the exact same pattern established in US-007.
acceptanceCriteria: ProjectsResource class with __init__(self, client) pattern
create_project(name, **kwargs) -> int; raises KanboardAPIError on False
get_project_by_id(project_id) -> Project; raises KanboardNotFoundError on None
get_project_by_name(name) -> Project; raises KanboardNotFoundError on None
get_project_by_identifier(identifier) -> Project; raises KanboardNotFoundError on None
get_project_by_email(email) -> Project; raises KanboardNotFoundError on None
get_all_projects() -> list[Project]
update_project(project_id, **kwargs) -> bool; raises KanboardAPIError on False
remove_project(project_id) -> bool
enable_project(project_id) -> bool
disable_project(project_id) -> bool
enable_project_public_access(project_id) -> bool
disable_project_public_access(project_id) -> bool
get_project_activity(project_id) -> list[dict]
get_project_activities(project_ids) -> list[dict]
Wired into KanboardClient as self.projects = ProjectsResource(self)
All methods fully type-hinted with typed returns
All public methods have docstrings
Unit tests for every method with pytest-httpx mocking
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Click application skeleton and global options
userStories.description: Build src/kanboard_cli/main.py with the root Click group, global options (--url, --token, --profile, --output, --verbose), config resolution wiring, and stub command group registrations for all resource categories.
acceptanceCriteria: cli() is a click.group with global options: --url, --token, --profile, --output/-o (Choice: table/json/csv/quiet), --verbose/-v
Config is resolved via KanboardConfig.resolve() and stored in ctx.obj
KanboardClient is created from resolved config and stored in ctx.obj
Config-less commands (e.g., future 'config init') work by catching KanboardConfigError

Stub command groups registered for: task, project, board, column, swimlane, category, comment, subtask, timer, user, me, tag, link, task-link, external-link, group, action, project-file, task-file, project-meta, task-meta, project-access, app, config, workflow
kanboard --help shows all command groups and global options
All public functions have docstrings
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Output formatters
userStories.description: Build src/kanboard_cli/formatters.py with format_output() supporting table (rich), JSON, CSV, and quiet (ID-only) output modes, plus format_success() for confirmation messages.
acceptanceCriteria: format_output(data, format, columns) normalizes dataclasses to dicts via dataclasses.asdict
table format: rich.table.Table with colored headers and auto-width columns
json format: json.dumps with indent=2; single object for one result, array for many
csv format: csv.DictWriter with proper escaping
quiet format: prints only 'id' field, one per line
format_success(message, format) outputs {'status': 'ok', 'message': ...} in JSON mode, '✓ {message}' otherwise
Handles empty data gracefully (empty table, empty array, no output in quiet)
Unit tests verify each format produces correct output
All public functions have docstrings and full type hints
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Task CLI commands
userStories.description: Implement src/kanboard_cli/commands/task.py with all 12 task subcommands: list, get, create, update, close, open, remove, search, move, move-to-project, duplicate, overdue. Wire into the CLI skeleton from US-009.
acceptanceCriteria: kanboard task list <project_id> [--status active|inactive] lists tasks
kanboard task get <task_id> shows task details
kanboard task create <project_id> <title> with options: --owner-id, --column-id, --swimlane-id, --due, --description, --color, --category-id, --score, --priority, --reference, --tag (multiple)
kanboard task update <task_id> with options: --title, --color, --due, --description, --owner-id, --category-id, --score, --priority, --reference, --tag (multiple)
kanboard task close <task_id> closes a task
kanboard task open <task_id> reopens a task
kanboard task remove <task_id> requires --yes confirmation
kanboard task search <project_id> <query> searches tasks
kanboard task move <task_id> --project-id --column-id --position --swimlane-id moves task position
kanboard task move-to-project <task_id> <project_id> with optional --swimlane-id, --column-id, --category-id, --owner-id
kanboard task duplicate <task_id> <project_id> with optional --swimlane-id, --column-id
kanboard task overdue [--project-id] shows overdue tasks
All commands support --output table/json/csv/quiet via inherited global option
CLI tests with CliRunner verify core commands across output formats
All public functions have docstrings
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Project CLI commands
userStories.description: Implement src/kanboard_cli/commands/project.py with all 8 project subcommands: list, get, create, update, remove, enable, disable, activity. Wire into the CLI skeleton from US-009.
acceptanceCriteria: kanboard project list shows all projects
kanboard project get <project_id> shows project details
kanboard project create <name> with options: --description, --owner-id, --identifier, --start-date, --end-date
kanboard project update <project_id> with options: --name, --description, --owner-id, --identifier
kanboard project remove <project_id> requires --yes confirmation
kanboard project enable <project_id> enables a project
kanboard project disable <project_id> disables a project
kanboard project activity <project_id> shows project activity feed
All commands support --output table/json/csv/quiet via inherited global option
CLI tests with CliRunner verify core commands across output formats
All public functions have docstrings
ruff check passes, pytest passes
